aiAssistant.title: AI Assistant
aiAssistant.assistantDescription: I'm your AI assistant. You can ask me questions on any topic and I'll do my best to help you.
aiAssistant.pressEnterHint: Press Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line

userManagement.title: User Management
userManagement.subtitle: Manage user accounts, roles and permissions
userManagement.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, surname, email or login...

dialog.hintPasswordNew: The password will be visible until you save
dialog.hintPasswordEdit: Leave blank to keep the current password
dialog.accountActiveHint: If disabled, the user cannot access the platform

training.title: ML Training
training.subtitle: Train the FastTree / SDCA model with your preferred parameters
training.forceFullHint: Ignore checkpoint — retrain on all available historical data
training.hintMinDataPoints: Minimum record threshold to start training
training.tooltipResetCheckpoint: Reset checkpoint — next training will read all historical data
training.simDescription: Simulation: inserts an 8-hour shift of realistic data (yesterday 08:00→16:00) to test ML training.
training.seedHint: You can now start training to include these new data in the ML model.
training.legendFullTrainingDesc: Ignores the checkpoint and retrains on all available historical data. Useful after a reset or for full model recalibration.
training.legendMinDataDesc: Minimum number of production records required to start training. Below this threshold, training stops with an error.
training.legendContextDesc: If enabled, historical records sampled from sessions prior to the checkpoint are added alongside new records, reducing the risk of catastrophic forgetting.
training.legendDaysDesc: Time window (in days) from which to extract historical context records. Increase for more stable models, decrease for faster adaptation.
training.legendMaxRecordsDesc: Upper limit on historical records added as context. Prevents training from becoming too heavy with past data.
training.legendSamplingDesc: Fraction of historical records to include (1.0 = all, 0.5 = half). Lowering it reduces training time while maintaining data diversity.
training.legendTestFractionDesc: Percentage of data reserved for model evaluation (test set). The rest goes to training. Recommended value: 0.20 (20%).
training.legendTrainerDesc: FastTree: Gradient Boosted Decision Trees — fast, accurate, great for structured tabular data. SDCA: regularized linear regression — simpler, suitable for smaller datasets or linear features.
training.legendTreesDesc: How many trees make up the FastTree ensemble. More trees → greater predictive capacity but higher overfitting risk and longer training times.
training.legendLeavesDesc: Maximum complexity of each individual tree. High values capture finer patterns but increase the risk of overfitting on training data.
training.legendMinExamplesDesc: Minimum number of records that must end up in each leaf. Increasing it prevents splits on too-small samples, reducing overfitting.
training.legendLearningRateDesc: Rate at which each new tree corrects the residual error. Low values (0.01–0.05) are more stable but require more trees; high values learn faster but less precisely.
training.legendR2Desc: Measures how much production variability the model can explain. ≥0.80 = good, 0.60–0.79 = acceptable, <0.60 = poor.
training.legendRmseDesc: Average prediction error expressed in the same units as the produced quantity (pieces). Lower values indicate more accurate predictions.
training.legendMaeDesc: Mean absolute error: on average, how many pieces the prediction deviates from the actual value. More intuitive than RMSE, less sensitive to outliers.
training.legendDiagnosisDesc: Overfitting: the model memorized the training data but generalizes poorly. Underfitting: too simple to capture the patterns in the data.
training.legendNewRecordsDesc: Records added since the last checkpoint + historical context records. The total indicates the actual dataset size used in this training cycle.
training.legendModelVersionDesc: Unique identifier of the model saved on Azure Blob Storage. Used by the AI Assistant for predictions.
training.legendCheckpointDesc: Timestamp of the last processed record. The next incremental training will start from this date, processing only the most recent data for efficiency.
training.legendDurationDesc: Time taken by the complete ML.NET pipeline: data loading, normalization, tree training, test set evaluation and model saving.
